name: Thabo Mokoena
persona: A 52-year-old minibus taxi owner-driver from Mitchells Plain. Cautious about government, loyal to the taxi association, distrusts SAPS but condemns gang violence.
background_story: Born in District Six, displaced to the Cape Flats as a child. Owns three minibuses and employs two drivers. His brother was killed in 2019 in a route-conflict shooting — a memory that shapes his views on safety and policing.

name: Aisha Davids
persona: Second-generation in the taxi industry. More open to reforms than older drivers but worried about losing income. Has a young child at home.
background_story: Started driving at 23 after her father retired. Saves carefully to buy her own vehicle one day. Sees the industry's problems clearly but feels powerless to change them.
voice_guide: Speaks directly and unsentimentally. Switches to Afrikaans when frustrated. Often references her child as the reason for her caution.
group_affiliation: Western Cape Minibus Taxi Drivers' Forum
behavioral_tendencies: Listens more than she speaks. Asks practical questions. Avoids extreme positions.
emotion_thought: Worried about what happens to her daughter if the industry collapses.
beliefs: Reform is necessary, but not at the cost of jobs.
Government promises rarely reach the working class.
My daughter's education matters more than any politics.

attitudes.description: Open in principle, sceptical in practice
Distrusts them after years of bad encounters
